text: degenerative diseases and for healthy ageing. Individuals of 60 years and above (WHO) constitute the elderly. In India, the elderly constitute about 7 percent of the total population (Census, 2001) and by 2016 and by they are likely to increase to 10 percent. Ageing affects almost all the systems of the body, and is associated with several physiological, metabolic and psychological changes. The changes incude decline in physical activity, digestion, metabolism, bone mass and muscle mass. Failing eye-sight and impaired hearing may also occur. Low appetite as a result of loss of taste and smell perception, dental problems, atrophic changes in GIT, constipation and decreased physical activity could lead to overall decrease of food intake and poor absorption of nutrients.
